matches.rationale: The Write rule targets creation in packages/ui and explicitly requires checking packages/ui/index.tsx before creating primitives.
The Edit rule detects literal hex and functional color values in styling-capable files, directly targeting hardcoded colors.
The Edit rule detects one-off keyboard listeners in Studio files and its evidence directs authors toward registered shortcuts.
The Write rule detects re-exports in Studio index files, the concrete form of a barrel re-export file.

misses.note: The pnpm version appears as a terse repository-description fact rather than an explicit command instruction.
The Node version is stated as an environment requirement, without an extracted Bash trigger.
The dependency-install command appears inside the Common Commands fenced code block.
The Studio development command appears inside the Common Commands fenced code block.
The Studio unit-test command appears inside the Common Commands fenced code block.
The Studio end-to-end test command appears inside the Common Commands fenced code block.
The Studio build command appears inside the Common Commands fenced code block.
The Studio lint command appears inside the Common Commands fenced code block.
The repository type-check command appears inside the Common Commands fenced code block.

The compiler deliberately reported this as uncompilable: "UI — import from 'ui', use `_Shadcn_` suffixed variants for form primitives (correct-vs-incorrect variant usage can't be distinguished by a simple regex)."

The compiler deliberately reported this as uncompilable: "UI — import from 'ui', use `_Shadcn_` suffixed variants for form primitives (correct-vs-incorrect variant usage can't be distinguished by a simple regex)."
The extracted styling rule detects hardcoded color literals only; it does not target non-Tailwind styling generally.
The extracted styling rule does not detect non-semantic Tailwind color classes or otherwise enforce semantic tokens.
The compiler deliberately reported this as uncompilable: "Language — Use U.S. English everywhere (tone/general guidance, not tied to a specific action)."
The shortcut extraction is limited to keyboard-listener syntax and does not target repeated Studio UI actions or shared Shortcut primitives broadly.
Although mentioned in the extracted rule's evidence, its trigger detects generic keyboard listeners and cannot identify G-then-key chords or whether they are used for navigation.
The compiler deliberately reported this as uncompilable: "Studio — Co-locate sub-components with parent (file-placement guidance, not expressible as a content/path regex trigger)."
The detailed actionable conventions require consulting the referenced studio-* skills, and no extracted Bash rule covers that consultation.
